title: orthiopteris campylura
intro: Orthiopteris campylura, also known as the mountain oak fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to Australia, particularly in New South Wales, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in the body, offering health benefits such as improved wound healing and reduced risk of illness. In herbal preparations, Orthiopteris campylura is often used in infusions or teas, as well as in topical applications such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
